Profile

Where Architecture Meets Artistry

Architect, artist, illustrator — a creative practice rooted in Dhaka, with vision shaped by Khulna and Rangpur.

01

Tasnim Irtiza Anzum

Architect & Artist

Tasnim Irtiza Anzum is a Dhaka-based architect and artist with a multidisciplinary practice spanning building design, interior architecture, digital illustration, and published visual art. Holding a Bachelor of Architecture from Khulna University (2010–16), he has built a distinctive creative identity at the intersection of spatial design and fine art.

Currently serving as Senior In-House Architect at Bombay Sweets & Company Ltd (2019–present), he has also contributed artistic work as a contract illustrator for PKSF, BRAC, TMSS, HSBC Bank, and AIRASTRA airlines.

Architecture

Exterior Building Projects

Residential towers, mixed-use complexes, and urban buildings designed across Bangladesh — 2016 to present.

02
Exterior · 01
Mrittika
Malotinagar, Bogra
A striking multi-storey residential building featuring a bold interplay of terracotta brick and white concrete panels, framed by lush vertical greenery on the facade.
Exterior · 02
Noor Palace
Housing Estate, Kushtia
An elegant residential tower blending warm brick tones with contemporary massing. Deep horizontal fins and recessed balconies create rhythm across the facade.
Exterior · 03
Ashike Residence
Khalishpur, Khulna
A thoughtfully designed triplex residence combining exposed brick with crisp white volumes, elevated terraces, and a compact yet dynamic street presence.
Exterior · 04
East Shaplabag Complex
East Shaplabag, Sylhet
A large-scale mixed-use building with a disciplined grid of bays, rooftop terraces, and a commercial podium — designed for a dense urban context in Sylhet.
Exterior · 05
Shadman City
Kushtia Sadar
A high-rise mixed-use tower with a transparent commercial base and a rhythmic curtain-wall facade — a landmark proposal for central Kushtia.
Interior Design

Interior Design Projects

Residential homes, corporate offices, and specialist spaces — designed with warmth, precision, and material intelligence.

03
Interior · 06
Sofana House
Shonadanga, Khulna
Warm timber panelling, generous bay windows, and a cohesive material palette bring comfort and character to this contemporary residential interior in Khulna.
Interior · 07
HSBC Branch Office
Gulshan, Dhaka
A sophisticated corporate office interior featuring custom mural art, geometric ceiling grids, deep plum accents, and curated lighting for the HSBC brand.
Interior · 08
Sabbir's Dental Chamber
Rupayan, Bashundhara, Dhaka
A clinical yet refined dental studio designed around a strong perspective corridor, layered glass partitions, and a clean white-and-blush material scheme.
Interior · 09
Hamid Residence
Katnerpara, Bogra
Neutral tones, dark-accented cabinetry, and layered lighting define this spacious residential interior — a calm, livable design for a family home in Bogra.
Interior · 10
Monwar Residence
Savar, Dhaka
Light oak woodwork, ambient ceiling lighting, and an open-plan layout give this Savar apartment a warm, modern character with architectural map-art detail.
Interior · 11
Reza Residence
Rangpur
A serene residential interior in Rangpur combining natural light, clean white surfaces, warm timber elements, and carefully curated artwork throughout.
Interior · 12
Salman Residence
Uttara, Dhaka
An expansive open-plan living and dining interior featuring pendant lighting, a sleek modular kitchen, and an effortless flow between living zones.
Interior · 13
Abrar Residence
Badda, Dhaka
A compact yet inventive residential interior weaving together a galley kitchen, staircase screen partition, and bold use of contrasting wood tones.
Fine Art

Pen & Ink Illustrations

Architectural illustrations and fine art works rendered in pen, ink, and digital media — celebrating heritage structures and the built environment.

04
Pen & Ink · Pen & Ink · Watercolour
Beauty Boarding & The Holy Kaaba
Two iconic subjects rendered in Anzum's signature pen-and-ink style: a lush Bengali courtyard scene and the sacred Kaaba — enriched with delicate watercolour washes.
Pen & Ink · Heritage Architecture of Dhaka
Curzon Hall · Ahsan Manjil · Raju Memorial
Three landmark structures of Dhaka illustrated with expressive line work and vibrant colour splashes — celebrating the architectural heritage of Bangladesh.
